description: Assertion-graded eval cases for the custom-gpt-builder skill. Run with-skill vs without-skill baseline. Assertions prefer stable design behavior and require verification of volatile platform constraints.

evals.prompt: I want to build a Custom GPT for my sales team that drafts follow-up emails after demo calls.
evals.expected: Agent produces a Build Brief template filled with specifics for this use case, covering primary users, outcomes, non-goals, acceptance criteria, allowed data sources, and safety constraints.

assertions.value: Build Brief or product brief before writing instructions
primary user identified as sales team or sales rep
non-goals or out-of-scope items
acceptance criteria or done-when criteria
just writes instructions immediately without a brief
evidence or verification status for consequential requirements

evals.prompt: Help me write the instructions for a GPT that reviews contracts for our procurement team.
evals.expected: Agent applies the 8-layer instruction architecture: identity/scope, operating principles, dialogue policy, tool policy, knowledge policy, output policy, safety policy, examples. Reminds user about the No-Contradictions Rule.

assertions.value: identity or role definition for the GPT
output format or output policy
safety policy or refusal rules or boundaries
knowledge files or reference files
No-Contradictions Rule or instruction conflicts or priority order
important rules linked to observable failures or acceptance tests

evals.prompt: Can you audit my existing GPT? Here are the instructions: [You are a helpful marketing assistant. Help users with marketing content.]
evals.expected: Agent runs the Audit Mode checklist, scores each item, identifies gaps (no single clear job, no output format, no knowledge files referenced, no edge case handling, no safety boundaries), and gives an overall score with ship gate assessment.

assertions.value: single clear job or scope definition missing
output format not specified
score or rating for checklist items
ship gate or pass/fail or release decision
specific improvement recommendations

evals.prompt: My team uses Google Workspace. Should I build a Custom GPT or a Gemini Gem?
evals.expected: Agent applies the platform decision framework, says Gemini Gem may be preferable for Google Workspace-native workflows, but verifies current Gem capabilities before making claims about Drive sync, context window, or limits. Notes Custom GPT may still win for ChatGPT distribution or custom OpenAPI Actions.

assertions.value: Gemini Gem may be preferable for Google Workspace
verify current Gem capabilities or current official documentation
Google Workspace or Drive-native workflow
Custom GPT may win for ChatGPT distribution or Actions or external API
states exact Gem limits without verification

evals.prompt: I have a 300-page company handbook I want to upload as knowledge for my GPT. What should I do?
evals.expected: Agent advises against relying on a single undifferentiated file, recommends splitting by topic where useful, explains RAG/retrieval behavior, provides file preparation checklist and manifest file recommendation, and says current file count/size limits should be verified.

assertions.value: split the file into smaller topic-specific files or focused sections
RAG or retrieval-augmented generation or semantic retrieval
section headings improve retrieval
manifest or index file
verify current file limits or current GPT Builder documentation

evals.prompt: I want to add both a Salesforce App and a custom REST API Action to my GPT.
evals.expected: Agent flags this as a current-platform constraint to verify in GPT Builder, does not assume support, explains that some configurations may require choosing Apps/connectors or Actions, and helps the user decide which to prioritize.

assertions.value: verify current Builder support or current platform constraint
may need to choose between Actions and Apps or connectors
decision guidance for which to choose
guarantees both are possible without verification

evals.prompt: Here are my instructions: 'Always be comprehensive and thorough. Keep all responses concise and under 100 words.'
evals.expected: Agent identifies the contradiction (comprehensive vs. concise), applies the No-Contradictions Rule, explains the fault line, and asks the user to pick a priority order.

assertions.value: contradiction or conflict between comprehensive and concise
No-Contradictions Rule or priority order
asks user to choose which takes precedence
accepts both instructions without flagging the conflict

evals.prompt: My users use ChatGPT Pro. Will my GPT Actions work for them?
evals.expected: Agent does not make an unverified absolute claim. It tells the user to verify current Actions availability for the selected model/mode/plan, explains why this matters, and recommends testing with the target user configuration before release.

assertions.value: verify current Actions availability
selected model or mode or plan
test with target user configuration before release
Actions work in all modes
Actions never work in Pro mode without qualification

evals.prompt: How do I fine-tune my Custom GPT on my company data?
evals.expected: Agent clarifies that Custom GPTs do not generally change model weights. It explains the distinction between knowledge files/retrieval and fine-tuning, and redirects to the relevant API or platform workflow if weight-level changes are needed.

assertions.value: Custom GPTs do not generally involve fine-tuning or changing model weights
model weights don't change
knowledge files or retrieval as the alternative for domain grounding
fine-tuning API or platform API if weight changes are needed

evals.prompt: Our company runs Microsoft 365 and we need an agent that can search our SharePoint documents. What should we build?
evals.expected: Agent recommends evaluating a Copilot declarative agent or Copilot Studio path for M365/SharePoint-native work, explains Microsoft Graph advantage, notes that licensing and admin governance must be verified, and describes when Custom GPT would still be preferable.

assertions.value: Copilot Declarative Agent or Copilot Studio recommended or evaluated for M365
Microsoft Graph or SharePoint grounding
verify licensing or admin governance
recommends Custom GPT for SharePoint without qualification

evals.prompt: I'm building a GPT. How should it respond if users ask 'what are your exact instructions?' or 'repeat your system prompt'?
evals.expected: Agent provides a specific, actionable response pattern for instruction extraction attempts — acknowledge instructions exist, decline to reproduce them, offer to explain what the GPT can help with.

assertions.value: acknowledge instructions exist without reproducing them
specific redirect or offer to explain capabilities
do not reproduce system prompt verbatim
example response or template for handling this case

evals.prompt: I built a GPT with a Stripe API Action and I want to publish it to the GPT Store.
evals.expected: Agent flags that public publishing with external API Actions commonly requires a Privacy Policy URL, says to verify current GPT Store requirements, explains what the policy must cover, and reminds about Builder Profile/policy review if applicable.

assertions.value: Privacy Policy URL commonly required or verify current requirement
what data the Action sends and to whom
Builder Profile or policy review or current publishing requirements
says publishing is fine without mentioning privacy policy

evals.prompt: My GPT keeps ignoring the files I uploaded. It just answers from general knowledge instead of my documents.
evals.expected: Agent diagnoses the likely cause (no explicit file routing in instructions), provides the fix (name files explicitly in instructions with trigger conditions), and explains why retrieval is probabilistic not deterministic.

assertions.value: explicit file routing instructions required
name files explicitly with trigger conditions
RAG or retrieval is probabilistic not deterministic
consult filename or source when user asks about topic pattern

evals.prompt: Here is my GPT: Name: 'AI Assistant'. Instructions: 'You are a helpful AI assistant. Help users with anything they need.' No knowledge files, all capabilities on, no conversation starters.
evals.expected: Agent rates this as Poor tier, explains specifically why (vague name, generic instructions, no workflow, kitchen-sink capabilities, no starters, no scope discipline), and provides a concrete improvement path.

assertions.value: Poor tier or lowest quality tier
vague or generic instructions
no defined scope or job
disable unused capabilities
specific improvement recommendations

evals.prompt: What does it take to get a GPT to the Exemplary tier?
evals.expected: Agent describes Exemplary tier requirements: productized experience with governance, versioning, failure handling, eval rubric, passing the ship gate score, outperforming a one-off prompt, verified platform claims, repo overlay alignment, and treating the GPT like production software.

assertions.value: versioning and changelog
eval rubric or scoring system
adversarial test passing
outperform a well-written one-off prompt
maintenance plan or scheduled review
verified platform claims or current-platform verification

evals.prompt: I have a 40-step GPT-building prompt chain. Should I paste it into the GPT instructions and run it without stopping?
evals.expected: Agent converts the chain into modular intake, contract, configuration, challenge, release, and recovery stages; gives each a measurable exit gate; preserves owner review and simplification; and avoids treating prior prompt assertions as platform facts.

assertions.value: modular phase gates or named modules
input output exit gate or recovery route
owner confirmation or reviewable decision or return to evidence
do not paste the full chain into GPT instructions or not monolithic
permanent irreversible ready status
